title: Crispy Duck Tostada
description: A twist on the classic Mexican dish, topped with crispy duck and fresh cilantro.
instructions: Season the duck breast with salt and black pepper.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the duck breast for 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the duck reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Let the duck rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Fry the corn tortillas for 30-45 seconds per side, or until crispy and golden brown.
Drain the tortillas on paper towels and season with tostada seasoning.
Assemble the tostadas by placing a piece of duck on each tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of cilantro, a squeeze of lime juice, and a sprinkle of queso fresco or feta cheese (if using).
